title: Encode Meaning inside Sentences
summary: Build and verify semantic text meaning in emergency advisory copy edit through prediction, parsed-structure inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain semantic text meaning from purpose and parsed structure.
Apply semantic text meaning while retaining prior HTML requirements.
Diagnose the named misconception in a changed content and interaction scenario.

steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim preserves semantic text meaning when the styling and content change? Scenario: emergency advisory copy edit.

content.title: emergency advisory copy edit evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves content meaning when visual styling and browser recovery are removed.

hints: Encode Meaning inside Sentences checkpoint 1: name the content relationship before choosing markup.
Encode Meaning inside Sentences checkpoint 1: compare source, parsed structure, and keyboard route.
Encode Meaning inside Sentences checkpoint 1: choose the smallest native relationship that survives changed styling.

options.text: Emergency advisory copy edit case 1: strong and em are merely longer spellings of bold and italic, so they can style any text without changing meaning.
Emergency advisory copy edit case 1: select inline elements from the meaning the sentence needs, and leave purely visual treatment to CSS.
Emergency advisory copy edit case 1: use strong for every heading because bold text is always more semantic.

steps.title: Read the emergency advisory copy edit relationship · theory

content.text: Text-level elements communicate linguistic meaning. Strong marks importance, em adds stress emphasis, dfn identifies a defining instance, abbr can connect an abbreviation to its expansion, cite names a creative work, and code identifies computer-language text. Their default appearance is incidental and may change. Apply it to emergency advisory copy edit.

emergency advisory copy edit phase 4.2: choose the text-level element matching that meaning
emergency advisory copy edit phase 4.3: preserve readable wording when markup is removed
emergency advisory copy edit phase 4.4: inspect the resulting semantics rather than default bold or italic pixels
